messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"512\" height=\"512\" viewBox=\"0 0 512 512\">\
<defs>\
  <linearGradient id=\"sky\" x1=\"0\" y1=\"0\" x2=\"0\" y2=\"1\">\
    <stop offset=\"0\" stop-color=\"#a7ddff\"/>\
    <stop offset=\"1\" stop-color=\"#ffffff\"/>\
  </linearGradient>\
  <radialGradient id=\"sunG\" cx=\"50%\" cy=\"20%\" r=\"40%\">\
    <stop offset=\"0\" stop-color=\"#fff9c4\"/>\
    <stop offset=\"1\" stop-color=\"#ffd07a\" stop-opacity=\"0.95\"/>\
  </radialGradient>\
</defs>\
<!-- background -->\
<rect width=\"512\" height=\"512\" fill=\"url(#sky)\"/>\
<!-- sun -->\
<g transform=\"translate(380,110)\">\
  <circle cx=\"0\" cy=\"0\" r=\"60\" fill=\"url(#sunG)\"/>\
  <g fill=\"#ffd38a\" opacity=\"0.35\">\
    <path d=\"M0,-100 L12,-30 L-12,-30 Z\"/>\
    <path d=\"M0,100 L12,30 L-12,30 Z\" transform=\"rotate(45)\"/>\
    <path d=\"M0,100 L12,30 L-12,30 Z\" transform=\"rotate(90)\"/>\
    <path d=\"M0,100 L12,30 L-12,30 Z\" transform=\"rotate(135)\"/>\
    <path d=\"M0,100 L12,30 L-12,30 Z\" transform=\"rotate(180)\"/>\
    <path d=\"M0,100 L12,30 L-12,30 Z\" transform=\"rotate(225)\"/>\
    <path d=\"M0,100 L12,30 L-12,30 Z\" transform=\"rotate(270)\"/>\
    <path d=\"M0,100 L12,30 L-12,30 Z\" transform=\"rotate(315)\"/>\
  </g>\
</g>\
<!-- ground suggestion -->\
<ellipse cx=\"260\" cy=\"420\" rx=\"220\" ry=\"40\" fill=\"#fff6e6\" opacity=\"0.6\"/>\
<!-- open cage (left-bottom) -->\
<g transform=\"translate(120,340)\">\
  <!-- cage dome -->\
  <path d=\"M-80,0 A80,80 0 0 1 80,0\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#6b6b6b\" stroke-width=\"6\" stroke-linecap=\"round\"/>\
  <!-- vertical bars -->\
  <g stroke=\"#6b6b6b\" stroke-width=\"4\" stroke-linecap=\"round\">\
    <path d=\"M-56,0 V-60\"/>\
    <path d=\"M-28,0 V- -68\"/>\
    <path d=\"M0,0 V-72\"/>\
    <path d=\"M28,0 V-68\"/>\
    <path d=\"M56,0 V-60\"/>\
  </g>\
  <!-- base -->\
  <rect x=\"-88\" y=\"0\" width=\"176\" height=\"10\" rx=\"4\" fill=\"#d9d9d9\" stroke=\"#6b6b6b\" stroke-width=\"3\"/>\
  <!-- open door (rotated outward) -->\
  <g transform=\"translate(80,0) rotate(25)\">\
    <rect x=\"0\" y=\"-60\" width=\"36\" height=\"60\" rx=\"3\" fill=\"#ffffff\" stroke=\"#6b6b6b\" stroke-width=\"4\"/>\
    <path d=\"M0,-60 L0,0\" stroke=\"#6b6b6b\" stroke-width=\"4\"/>\
  </g>\
</g>\
<!-- broken chain pieces near cage and toward bird -->\
<g stroke=\"#7a7a7a\" stroke-width=\"8\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" fill=\"none\">\
  <!-- attached to cage base -->\
  <path d=\"M200,370 C220,360 240,350 260,340\"/>\
  <!-- link arcs, one gap to show broken -->\
  <path d=\"M262,338 A14,14 0 1 1 290,324\"/>\
  <path d=\"M300,318 A14,14 0 1 1 330,304\"/>\
  <!-- broken gap (gap between links) -->\
  <path d=\"M340,292 A14,14 0 0 1 370,278\" stroke-dasharray=\"40 10\"/>\
  <path d=\"M380,272 C390,260 410,240 420,220\"/>\
</g>\
<!-- bird in flight (center) -->\
<g transform=\"translate(240,220)\">\
  <!-- left wing -->\
  <path d=\"M-80,10 C-20,-40 20,-60 80,-10 C40,-30 0,-20 -20,-5 C-40,10 -60,30 -80,10 Z\" fill=\"#ffffff\" stroke=\"#2b4966\" stroke-width=\"3\"/>\
  <!-- right wing -->\
  <path d=\"M0,0 C40,-10 100,-30 160,-60 C120,-30 90,-10 60,5 C40,15 20,20 0,0 Z\" fill=\"#ffffff\" stroke=\"#2b4966\" stroke-width=\"3\"/>\
  <!-- body -->\
  <ellipse cx=\"20\" cy=\"20\" rx=\"40\" ry=\"26\" fill=\"#ffffff\" stroke=\"#2b4966\" stroke-width=\"3\"/>\

**Creating an SVG for Freedom**

I need to design an SVG that represents "freedom" without using any text or numbers. I'm envisioning imagery like a bird flying, an open cage, and broken chains. The canvas will be 512x512, and it should be simple yet impactful. I think about including a bird in flight with a broken chain nearby, sun rays in the background, and a gradient from light blue to white for the sky. Everything must be created using shapes, paths, and fills only.**Sketching an SVG for Freedom**

I’m working on an SVG string that’s 512x512 in size. I’m setting the viewBox to 0 0 512 512 and using definitions for gradients. The background will have a linear gradient, with the sun drawn as a circle using a radial gradient, complete with rays made from rotated rectangles or paths. 

I’m picturing an open cage at the bottom right, with a broken chain linked to a bird's leg, avoiding any text and keeping it simple. I'll ensure the bird is a silhouette, filled in white with a slight black stroke.**Designing a Bird Path**

I’m working on crafting a stylized flying bird using cubic Bezier paths. I think I should simplify it by using two wing shapes along with a bird body shape. I’ll create a group with a white fill and a dark blue thin stroke. 

For the bird body, I’ll use a small ellipse, and then I’ll add two large curved paths for the wings and some tail feathers. I also plan to depict broken chain links as circles with a dark grey stroke and one open link shown by an arc.**Drafting SVG for Cage**

I’m planning to draw a cage using a dome shape made from an arc path, with vertical bars and a semicircular base that has an open door as a rotated rectangle. For colors, I’ll use a sky gradient from light blue to pale. The bird will be white, possibly with a soft drop shadow, though I might avoid filter complexity. 

I need to format this SVG as JSON, ensuring proper escaping for quotes. The final output will only include the SVG string within a single field labeled "svg."**Writing SVG with JSON Formatting**

I’m preparing to write the SVG, using single double quotes for attributes and ensuring I properly escape them in the JSON format. The SVG will include defined gradients for the sky and sun, along with a rectangle for the background. I plan to draw the sun, rays, bird shapes, and the cage structure using paths and polygons.

I’ll make sure the SVG string is tidy and avoid including comments in the final JSON output. Lastly, I'll form the full SVG, ensuring I keep it simple enough to avoid invalid paths, and then assemble everything into a properly formatted JSON object with the SVG value escaped.

You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.

  "svg": "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"512\" height=\"512\" viewBox=\"0 0 512 512\">\
  <defs>\
    <linearGradient id=\"sky\" x1=\"0\" y1=\"0\" x2=\"0\" y2=\"1\">\
      <stop offset=\"0%\" stop-color=\"#76c7f0\"/>\
      <stop offset=\"60%\" stop-color=\"#bfe9ff\"/>\
      <stop offset=\"100%\" stop-color=\"#fff7e0\"/>\
    </linearGradient>\
    <radialGradient id=\"sunG\" cx=\"50%\" cy=\"80%\" r=\"40%\">\
      <stop offset=\"0%\" stop-color=\"#fff7b2\"/>\
      <stop offset=\"60%\" stop-color=\"#ffd36b\"/>\
      <stop offset=\"100%\" stop-color=\"#ffb74d\"/>\
    </radialGradient>\
  </defs>\
  <!-- background sky -->\
  <rect x=\"0\" y=\"0\" width=\"512\" height=\"512\" fill=\"url(#sky)\"/>\
  <!-- sun near horizon -->\
  <circle cx=\"256\" cy=\"380\" r=\"90\" fill=\"url(#sunG)\" opacity=\"0.95\"/>\
  <!-- soft sun glow -->\
  <circle cx=\"256\" cy=\"380\" r=\"140\" fill=\"#fff2c8\" opacity=\"0.18\"/>\
  <!-- faint sun rays -->\
  <g fill=\"#fff7c9\" opacity=\"0.25\">\
    <path d=\"M256 240 L266 120 L246 120 Z\" transform=\"rotate(12 256 256)\"/>\

    <path d=\"M256 240 L266 120 L246 120 Z\" transform=\"rotate(30 256 256)\"/>\
    <path d=\"M256 240 L266 120 L246 120 Z\" transform=\"rotate(-30 256 256)\"/>\
  </g>\
  <!-- broken chain across lower middle -->\
  <g stroke=\"#555\" stroke-width=\"14\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" fill=\"none\">\
    <!-- left links -->\
    <ellipse cx=\"80\" cy=\"360\" rx=\"34\" ry=\"20\" transform=\"rotate(-25 80 360)\"/>\
    <ellipse cx=\"150\" cy=\"345\" rx=\"34\" ry=\"20\" transform=\"rotate(-16 150 345)\"/>\
    <ellipse cx=\"220\" cy=\"330\" rx=\"34\" ry=\"20\" transform=\"rotate(-8 220 330)\"/>\
    <!-- right links -->\
    <ellipse cx=\"292\" cy=\"315\" rx=\"34\" ry=\"20\" transform=\"rotate(8 292 315)\"/>\
    <ellipse cx=\"362\" cy=\"330\" rx=\"34\" ry=\"20\" transform=\"rotate(18 362 330)\"/>\
    <ellipse cx=\"432\" cy=\"350\" rx=\"34\" ry=\"20\" transform=\"rotate(30 432 350)\"/>\
    <!-- broken middle gap and jagged ends -->\
  </g>\
  <!-- jagged break pieces to imply snapped chain -->\
  <g fill=\"#555\">\
    <path d=\"M245 323 l12 -8 l6 10 l-12 8 z\" transform=\"rotate(-10 250 330)\"/>\
    <path d=\"M267 312 l-12 8 l-6 -10 l12 -8 z\" transform=\"rotate(8 266 312)\"/>\

    <circle cx=\"260\" cy=\"328\" r=\"3\" fill=\"#555\"/>\
    <circle cx=\"252\" cy=\"318\" r=\"2.5\" fill=\"#666\"/>\
  </g>\
  <!-- stylized bird silhouette flying upward -->\
  <g transform=\"translate(60,10) scale(1.2)\">\
    <path d=\"M160 170 C200 120 250 100 300 130 C340 150 390 110 430 120 C380 140 350 130 320 150 C300 165 280 170 260 165 C240 160 220 150 200 150 C180 150 160 160 140 170 C120 180 110 200 120 200 C130 200 140 190 160 170 Z\" fill=\"#0b2a3a\"/>\
    <!-- trailing smaller wing to suggest motion -->\
    <path d=\"M220 190 C250 160 290 150 330 170 C300 160 270 170 240 190 C230 200 220 200 220 190 Z\" fill=\"#093240\" opacity=\"0.9\"/>\
  </g>\
  <!-- subtle horizon line to give openness -->\
  <path d=\"M40 400 C140 370 372 370 472 400\" stroke=\"#ffdca3\" stroke-width=\"6\" fill=\"none\" opacity=\"0.6\" stroke-linecap=\"round\"/>\
  <!-- suggestion of distant birds (small V shapes) -->\
  <g stroke=\"#0b2a3a\" stroke-width=\"4\" fill=\"none\" stroke-linecap=\"round\">\
    <path d=\"M340 90 q12 -10 24 0\" />\

message.reasoning:
**Creating visual representation of freedom**

I need to make an SVG that illustrates the word "freedom" without using any text. I can consider graphics like a broken chain, an open birdcage with a silhouette of a bird, or a dove with its wings spread. I want to keep things simple in a 512x512 format and use shapes like paths and polygons. The colors I choose should symbolize freedom, such as a blue sky gradient. I’ll focus on creating a valid JSON string with just one field for the SVG content.**Designing symbols of freedom**

I'm working on creating a graphic symbolizing freedom using simple elements. I envision an open cage with a door swung wide and broken chain links. A broken chain across the center can emphasize the idea, with two jagged links. I plan to depict a bird flying upward from the broken chain for simplicity. The bird can be a classic silhouette, while the chain consists of linked ovals. I'll include sun rays behind the bird, using clear strokes. The SVG will be crafted with a viewBox of 512x512 and a gradient for the sky.**Crafting the SVG shapes**

I’m planning to create an SVG with elements like dark gray ellipses or paths, ensuring one link is broken with a jagged shape. I'm considering a subtle open cage in the bottom-left, but I might just illustrate a broken shackle instead. I need to keep things simple and avoid any text. For the background, I’ll use a rectangle with a linear gradient, while the sun will be a radial gradient circle. The bird will have stylized coordinates shaped like a swallow using cubic Bezier curves for a dynamic look.**Designing the bird and chains**

I think it’s better to position the bird at the center top. I can try crafting its silhouette from memory using an outlined path. It would look like a bird with two wings, so I'll refine the coordinates to give it that shape. For the chain, I’ll draw several ellipses that are rotated and connected, and for the broken link, I should use a dashed line or create two separated links with jagged ends. An open hand releasing the bird could be a nice extra touch too!**Creating the freedom symbol**

I’m focusing on a simple composition that combines a bird, a broken chain, an open sky, and the sun to symbolize freedom. The bird will fly from the bottom left to the top right, so I’ll craft the SVG path for the bird with its wings shaped as a filled black path. The chain will stretch along the lower center with separated links and jagged pieces. I need to ensure the JSON string is properly formatted with escaped quotes for readability. Let’s finalize that!

{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"512\" height=\"512\" viewBox=\"0 0 512 512\">\
<defs>\
  <linearGradient id=\"sky\" x1=\"0\" y1=\"0\" x2=\"0\" y2=\"1\">\
    <stop offset=\"0\" stop-color=\"#87CEEB\" />\
    <stop offset=\"1\" stop-color=\"#FFFFFF\" />\
  </linearGradient>\
  <radialGradient id=\"sun\" cx=\"50%\" cy=\"78%\" r=\"45%\">\
    <stop offset=\"0\" stop-color=\"#FFD36E\" stop-opacity=\"1\"/>\
    <stop offset=\"1\" stop-color=\"#FFD36E\" stop-opacity=\"0\"/>\
  </radialGradient>\
</defs>\
<!-- background -->\
<rect width=\"100%\" height=\"100%\" fill=\"url(#sky)\"/>\
<!-- sun glow -->\
<circle cx=\"256\" cy=\"400\" r=\"140\" fill=\"url(#sun)\"/>\
<!-- rays -->\
<g fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#FFD36E\" stroke-opacity=\"0.25\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-width=\"18\">\
  <path d=\"M256 400 L256 80\" />\
  <path d=\"M256 400 L320 85\" />\
  <path d=\"M256 400 L192 85\" />\
  <path d=\"M256 400 L370 130\" />\
  <path d=\"M256 400 L142 130\" />\
</g>\
<!-- large bird (silhouette) -->\
<path d=\"M120 280 C160 220 230 210 300 250 C260 230 220 240 180 260 C210 230 250 200 330 180 C300 210 260 250 200 280 C170 290 140 300 120 280 Z\" fill=\"#0d3b66\"/>\
<!-- smaller birds (flying upwards) -->\
<path d=\"M360 200 q12 -12 24 0\" stroke=\"#0d3b66\" stroke-width=\"6\" fill=\"none\" stroke-linecap=\"round\"/>\
<path d=\"M402 230 q14 -16 28 0\" stroke=\"#0d3b66\" stroke-width=\"6\" fill=\"none\" stroke-linecap=\"round\"/>\
<path d=\"M320 190 q10 -8 20 0\" stroke=\"#0d3b66\" stroke-width=\"5\" fill=\"none\" stroke-linecap=\"round\"/>\
<!-- broken chain links (symbolizing release) -->\
<g stroke=\"#6b6b6b\" stroke-width=\"18\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" fill=\"none\">\
  <ellipse cx=\"120\" cy=\"410\" rx=\"60\" ry=\"30\" transform=\"rotate(-25 120 410)\" />\
  <ellipse cx=\"190\" cy=\"350\" rx=\"60\" ry=\"30\" transform=\"rotate(-25 190 350)\" />\
</g>\
<!-- gap and fragments to suggest break -->\
<circle cx=\"150\" cy=\"375\" r=\"6\" fill=\"#6b6b6b\"/>\
<circle cx=\"162\" cy=\"362\" r=\"4\" fill=\"#8b8b8b\"/>\
<circle cx=\"138\" cy=\"388\" r=\"3\" fill=\"#9a9a9a\"/>\
<!-- subtle ground line to anchor composition -->\
<path d=\"M40 460 C120 420 392 420 472 460\" stroke=\"#f0e6d6\" stroke-opacity=\"0.6\" stroke-width=\"6\" fill=\"none\" stroke-linecap=\"round\"/>\
</svg>"
}
message.reasoning:
**Designing SVG Composition**

The system requires valid JSON output, so I need to ensure my SVG meets that format. For a simple design, I’m thinking about a background gradient that goes from light blue at the top to white near the horizon. I could depict a rising sun at the center with rays spreading upward, along with a stylized bird silhouette flying upward to the right. There’s also the idea of a broken chain or an open birdcage with birds escaping. Simplicity is key here!**Creating SVG Design**

I’m focusing on a composition that avoids any text or numbers. For the background, I envision a radial gradient sun at the bottom center with soft rays. A large bird silhouette will be positioned towards the center-left, flying upwards. I want to add broken chain arcs with fragments in the lower-left corner and small birds soaring upward in the lower-right. To achieve simplicity, I’ll use paths for the birds and chains, ensuring they look like links with rounded edges. Finally, I may add small circular debris to enhance the visual interest while keeping it all straightforward.**Crafting SVG Markup**

For my SVG design, I'm creating a setup with a 512x512 size. I'll define a linear gradient for the sky that transitions from light blue to white. There’s also a radial gradient for the sun to give it a soft glow. I'll make a rectangle that covers the background and a circle for the sun. For the sun's rays, instead of simple rectangles, I might use paths with polygons for a cleaner look. Finally, I want to draw a stylized dove silhouette using Bezier curves. I need to figure out those exact coordinates for it!**Designing Bird Silhouettes and Chains**

I’m thinking of creating two birds: a larger central one and two smaller ones represented by simple 'V' shaped paths. The large bird silhouette will have a more complex path, but I have a simpler one from memory that should work: it needs to distinctly look like a bird. For the smaller flying birds, I'm considering using easy paths to depict them as well. As for the broken chain effect, I’ll draw two ovals at the lower left, utilizing a stroke-dasharray to give the appearance of missing segments and some small fragments. Simple is key here!**Finalizing SVG Chain and Birds**

I’m focused on drawing a chain link with elliptical paths, using a width of 18 and a rounded cap. The broken piece will have two arcs showing a gap. The links will be placed in the lower-left corner. For the birds, I’ve got a stylized silhouette and small birds made from simple paths. I'll ensure everything fits neatly, using a grey color for the chain and contrasting dark blue for the birds. The final SVG will be assembled in JSON format while being careful about escaping characters and ensuring no invalid comments are present.**Preparing Final JSON for SVG**

I need to create the final JSON output, ensuring that the quotes in the SVG string are double quotes. The outer JSON value will enclose the string, but I'll need to escape the internal double quotes properly. I have to manually prepare the JSON, which should look like this: { "svg": "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"512\" height=\"512\" viewBox=\"0 0 512 512\">... </svg>" }. I’ll craft the complete SVG text first and then escape those necessary double quotes before presenting it.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

{ "svg": "string" }

{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='512' height='512' viewBox='0 0 512 512'>
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id='sky' x1='0' y1='0' x2='0' y2='1'>
      <stop offset='0%' stop-color='#B3E5FC'/>
      <stop offset='60%' stop-color='#E1F5FE'/>
      <stop offset='100%' stop-color='#FFFFFF'/>
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id='sunGrad' cx='0.8' cy='0.15' r='0.18'>
      <stop offset='0%' stop-color='#FFF59D'/>
      <stop offset='100%' stop-color='rgba(255,235,59,0.6)'/>
    </radialGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- background sky -->
  <rect x='0' y='0' width='512' height='512' fill='url(#sky)' />

  <!-- sun -->
  <circle cx='410' cy='70' r='60' fill='url(#sunGrad)' />

  <!-- soft clouds -->
  <g opacity='0.9' fill='#FFFFFF'>
    <ellipse cx='360' cy='120' rx='50' ry='20' />
    <ellipse cx='320' cy='130' rx='40' ry='18' />
    <ellipse cx='395' cy='135' rx='30' ry='12' />

    <ellipse cx='180' cy='100' rx='60' ry='24' />
    <ellipse cx='140' cy='115' rx='42' ry='18' />
  </g>

  <!-- ground hint -->
  <rect x='0' y='440' width='512' height='72' fill='rgba(240,248,255,0.6)' />

  <!-- open cage (left, broken door) -->
  <g stroke='#6b4f2b' stroke-width='6' stroke-linecap='round' fill='none'>
    <!-- cage frame -->
    <path d='M70 420 Q70 300 150 300 L200 300 Q230 300 230 330 L230 420 Q230 450 200 450 L100 450 Q70 450 70 420 Z' fill='rgba(255,255,255,0.02)' stroke-linejoin='round'/>

    <!-- vertical bars (some broken) -->
    <g stroke='#6b4f2b' stroke-width='4'>
      <path d='M100 320 L100 420' />
      <path d='M120 320 L120 360' />
      <path d='M140 320 L140 420' />
      <path d='M160 320 L160 360' />
      <path d='M180 320 L180 420' />
    </g>

    <!-- hinge / broken door swung open -->
    <g transform='translate(230,340) rotate(-30)'>
      <rect x='0' y='-30' width='90' height='90' rx='10' fill='none' stroke='#6b4f2b' stroke-width='6'/>
      <!-- broken corner pieces -->
      <path d='M10 -10 L0 -22' stroke='#6b4f2b' stroke-width='5' stroke-linecap='round'/>
      <path d='M40 38 L52 50' stroke='#6b4f2b' stroke-width='5' stroke-linecap='round'/>
    </g>

    <!-- small chain links detached -->
    <g stroke='#8a5a3c' stroke-width='4' fill='none'>
      <ellipse cx='250' cy='300' rx='10' ry='6' transform='rotate(20 250 300)' />
      <ellipse cx='280' cy='285' rx='12' ry='7' transform='rotate(30 280 285)' />
      <ellipse cx='305' cy='270' rx='9' ry='5' transform='rotate(10 305 270)' />
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- birds flying out (silhouettes) -->
  <g stroke='#12324b' stroke-width='6' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round' fill='none'>
    <!-- near cage, small -->
    <path d='M240 300 C255 290 270 285 285 275' />

    <!-- mid bird, larger and rising -->
    <path d='M260 260 C285 235 320 220 350 200' />

    <!-- highest bird, open wings -->
    <path d='M320 200 C340 180 370 170 400 150' />

    <!-- stylized wing florets -->
    <path d='M270 280 C290 270 305 260 320 250' opacity='0.9' />
  </g>

  <!-- feather fragments drifting -->
  <g fill='#1b3a4b' opacity='0.9'>
    <ellipse cx='290' cy='255' rx='6' ry='3' transform='rotate(-25 290 255)' />
    <ellipse cx='330' cy='225' rx='5' ry='2.5' transform='rotate(-15 330 225)' />
    <ellipse cx='360' cy='190' rx='7' ry='3' transform='rotate(-10 360 190)' />
  </g>

  <!-- subtle motion arcs to suggest upward movement -->
  <g stroke='rgba(26,42,59,0.25)' stroke-width='3' fill='none' stroke-linecap='round'>
    <path d='M250 310 C280 290 300 260 330 230' />
    <path d='M280 290 C310 270 340 240 380 200' />
  </g>
</svg>"
}

I want an open cage in the lower left with a broken door and a few broken chain links, allowing three birds to fly out, symbolizing freedom. Maybe I could add a ribbon or an open hand? I need to ensure everything is simple and stuck to shapes and paths while adhering to specific attribute requirements for JSON formatting.**Creating SVG Details**

To minimize escaping in my SVG, I plan to use single quotes for attributes while ensuring the JSON string still follows the double quote requirement. I’m thinking of producing an SVG element that defines gradients, draws a full background rectangle with a sky gradient, and adds a pale yellow sun in the top right corner. I also want to create clouds from white ellipses, include a rounded-corner birdcage, broken door, and three simple flying birds using cubic Bezier curves. Keeping it text-free is key!**Designing the Birdcage SVG**

I'm positioning the cage around x=60, y=310, with a width and height of 140. The bars will be vertical lines spaced evenly in the design. For the door, I plan to draw an arc swung open to the right and create small rotated ovals for chain links near the hinge. The birds will have a classic "V" shape with dark navy color, and I need to write the SVG and produce the JSON without escaping issues. I’ve got this!**Finalizing the SVG Creation**

I'm considering using path coordinates to simulate an open door, which might be simpler. I could draw the door as a path starting at x=180, y=340, shaped and rotated properly. For chain links, I'll add small ellipses at the top of the cage, and I’ll include broken bar pieces using small lines. The birds will be three paths with strokes, and I might add motion lines or small feathers to enhance the effect. Now, I’ll craft the final SVG content and put it in JSON!

You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

message.reasoning:
**Interpreting the drawing**

I'm trying to figure out what the drawing shows. It seems to depict two squares with rounded corners, one large on the left and one small on the right, possibly connected by a ring. That makes me think it could represent luggage tags or even toast! There's also motion lines, a sun, and clouds. I'm considering if it might be a kite or even a paper airplane. Maybe it's a suitcase being tossed? Another possibility is that it's two pieces of toast popping out of a toaster!**Analyzing the shapes**

I'm considering that the left rounded rectangle might represent a toaster, with the small square being toast popping out. But wait, aren’t toasters usually vertical? I could see it as a toaster with toast flying out, and the small shape has a diagonal line that looks like a bite mark. Alternatively, could it symbolize luggage and a backpack being thrown? The lines on the large rectangle resemble grill marks on bread. I wonder if it’s more about a sandwich with filling instead? The sun and clouds might just serve as a background to hint at the toast popping up.**Identifying the connectors**

I'm seeing that the small connectors between the big and small shapes look like springs, which makes me think they could represent a hinge or possibly a toaster's ejecting spring. It seems like the loops might also signify a heating coil. It's challenging to decide between “toast” and “toaster” for this interpretation, but I think “toast” is more likely. I should order my guesses with the highest confidence first, starting with “toast,” then maybe “bread.” I'll create a list of related terms to explore further: toast, toaster, bread, slice, sandwich, and poptart. Hmm, what about “toastpop”?**Finalizing word guesses**

I need to ensure that I provide one-word guesses in US English, avoiding repeats. My goal is to come up with 20 words, ordered by confidence. So I’ll start with “toast,” then “toaster,” “bread,” “slice,” “sandwich,” “meal,” “breakfast,” “pastry,” “bagel,” “croissant,” “biscuit,” “poptart,” “crust,” “crumb,” “grill,” “oven,” “appliance,” “kitchen,” and “breakfastfood” — though I might need to avoid odd words. I want them all to be real words without punctuation or numbers. Let’s finalize this list!**Creating the final list**

I’m adjusting my list now, replacing one word with “bagel” and making sure everything is a single word with no repeats. The final words I’m considering are “toast,” “toaster,” “bread,” “slice,” “sandwich,” “bagel,” “poptart,” “croissant,” “biscuit,” “muffin,” “waffle,” “pastry,” “loaf,” “oven,” “kitchen,” “grill,” “panini,” “crouton,” “crumb,” and “breakfast.” I need to stick with the singular “crumb.” It looks good overall, so I’ll prepare this final output in JSON format!
